nodes.label: Neurotoxicity Following Exposure to Micro and Nanoplastics

data.title: Neurotoxicity Following Exposure to Micro and Nanoplastics
data.exposure_context: neurotoxic implications of nanoplastics across various species and in vitro settings
data.notes: The review includes studies published between 2015 and 2024.

data.identification_capability: confirmed absorption and presence of particles in freeze-dried tissues

data.label: inflammation
data.notes: The paper reports upregulated TNF-α and IL-6 in mouse brain tissues exposed to PS-NH2.

data.label: other
data.notes: Apoptosis (neuron cell death): caspase-3, a marker for neuron cell apoptosis, showed increased signals in mouse brain tissues exposed to PS-NH2.

nodes.label: lowered survival rates and decreased average lifespan

data.label: lowered survival rates and decreased average lifespan
data.notes: Caenorhabditis elegans exposed to spherical polystyrene microplastics showed lowered survival rates and decreased average lifespan.

data.label: brain
data.vulnerability_notes: Mouse brain tissues exposed to PS-NH2 showed increased caspase-3 signals and upregulated TNF-α and IL-6.

data.label: liver
data.vulnerability_notes: Mouse liver showed dose-dependent alterations in energy metabolism, including reduced ATP levels and elevated LDH activity.

data.label: kidney
data.vulnerability_notes: Mouse kidney is reported as a particle-accumulation site after oral polystyrene microplastic exposure.

data.label: gut
data.vulnerability_notes: Gut or digestive tract is repeatedly reported as a particle-accumulation site across mouse and aquatic invertebrate studies.

data.label: hemolymph
data.vulnerability_notes: Hemolymph or hemolymphatic sinuses are reported as particle-detection sites in bivalve studies.

data.assumptions_notes: The conclusions list ingestion among exposure routes; particles can be taken in by aquatic beings and mammals.

data.population_context: aquatic organisms (fish, mussels, clams, crustacean larvae)
data.assumptions_notes: Aquatic species were exposed to microplastics suspended in their surrounding water; particles were recovered from gut lumen, digestive gland, gills, and hemolymph.
Earthworms were exposed to LDPE particles within artificial soil; uptake was confirmed and skin damage observed.

data.label: freshwater
data.subcompartment: freshwater exposure medium for zebra mussels and Asian clams

data.notes: During the initial week of exposure, 5 μm polystyrene particle concentrations stabilized at approximately 0.2 mg/g in liver.
During the initial week of exposure, 5 μm polystyrene particle concentrations stabilized at approximately 1.0 mg/g in kidney.
During the initial week of exposure, 5 μm polystyrene particle concentrations stabilized at approximately 1.4 mg/g in gut.
Polyethylene particle uptake was confirmed after 14-28 days at 1.5 g/kg soil.

Dreissena polymorpha exposed to pristine polystyrene microbeads of 1 μm and 10 μm at concentrations of 1 and 4 × 10^6 MPs/L for six days showed accumulation of particles within the gut lumen and further movement to tissues and hemolymph.
Mouse brain tissues exposed to PS-NH2 showed increased caspase-3 signals.
Mouse brain tissues exposed to PS-NH2 showed TNF-α and IL-6 upregulation.

Caenorhabditis elegans exposed to spherical polystyrene microplastics (0.1-5 μm, 1 mg/L) showed movement effects, lowered survival rates, decreased average lifespan, and neuronal gene-expression changes; the review notes no direct proof of ingestion.

nodes.label: Primary study [41]: PS-NH2 neurotoxicity in mouse brain

data.title: Primary study [41]: PS-NH2 neurotoxicity in mouse brain
data.notes: Primary study cited by the review. Mouse brain tissues exposed to PS-NH2 showed increased caspase-3 signals and upregulated TNF-alpha and IL-6.

nodes.label: Primary study [42]: polystyrene microplastics in mice (oral ...

data.title: Primary study [42]: polystyrene microplastics in mice (oral gavage)
data.exposure_context: chronic 30-day oral gavage, 5 and 20 um polystyrene microplastics
data.notes: The solely published mice in vivo study cited by the review; particles accumulated in gut, liver, and kidneys.

nodes.label: Primary study [85]: LDPE neurotoxicity in earthworms (Eiseni...

data.title: Primary study [85]: LDPE neurotoxicity in earthworms (Eisenia fetida)
data.exposure_context: low-density polyethylene 100-200 um, 0.1-1.5 g/kg soil, up to 28 days
data.notes: Primary study cited by the review; confirmed polyethylene uptake and oxidative stress markers.

nodes.label: Primary study [86]: polystyrene microbeads in zebra mussels ...

data.title: Primary study [86]: polystyrene microbeads in zebra mussels (Dreissena polymorpha)
data.exposure_context: pristine polystyrene microbeads 1 and 10 um, 1 and 4 x 10^6 MPs/L, six days
data.notes: Primary study cited by the review; particles accumulated but caused no genotoxic effects.

nodes.label: Primary studies [83,84]: spherical polystyrene exposure in C...

data.title: Primary studies [83,84]: spherical polystyrene exposure in C. elegans
data.exposure_context: spherical polystyrene microplastics, 0.1-5 um, 1 mg/L culture medium
data.notes: Primary studies cited by the review; movement, survival, lifespan, neuronal gene, and oxidative-stress findings were summarized, with no direct proof of ingestion.

data.label: Mouse
data.notes: Adult mice exposed to polystyrene microplastics (5 and 20 um) and PS-NH2.

data.label: C. elegans
data.notes: Exposed to spherical polystyrene microplastics (0.1-5 um) at 1 mg/L; excitotoxicity, lowered survival, decreased lifespan.

data.label: Earthworm
data.notes: Exposed to LDPE particles (100-200 um) in artificial soil; skin damage and oxidative stress markers.

data.label: Zebra mussel
data.notes: Exposed to polystyrene microbeads (1 and 10 um); particle accumulation, increased dopamine, no genotoxicity.

nodes.label: The exposure to polystyrene microplastics led to the absorpt...

data.text: The exposure to polystyrene microplastics led to the absorption and presence of particles in the mice's gut, liver, and kidneys, confirmed via fluorescence spectrometer analysis of freeze-dried tissues.

nodes.label: Mouse brain tissues exposed to PS-NH2 showed increased caspa...

data.text: Mouse brain tissues exposed to PS-NH2 showed increased caspase-3 signals, a marker for neuron cell apoptosis.

nodes.label: Cytokines like TNF-alpha and IL-6 were upregulated in these ...

data.text: Cytokines like TNF-alpha and IL-6 were upregulated in these tissues, hinting at inflammation caused by cytokine presence.

nodes.label: Upon analyzing and quantifying polyethylene particles, their...

data.text: Upon analyzing and quantifying polyethylene particles, their uptake (after 14-28 days at 1.5 g/kg soil) was confirmed.

nodes.label: This led to the accumulation of particles within the gut lum...

data.text: This led to the accumulation of particles within the gut lumen and further movement to tissues and hemolymph, as observed with confocal microscopy.

nodes.label: Caenorhabditis elegans were subjected to five different sphe...

data.text: Caenorhabditis elegans were subjected to five different spherical polystyrene microplastic sizes (0.1-5 μm) in a culture medium at 1 mg/L concentration. This led to excitotoxicity affecting their movement, lowered survival rates, and decreased average lifespan, with the most pronounced effects observed with exposure to 1.0 μm size particles.
